source_pr.title: store/tikv: call Next() after copIterator closed lead to goroutine leak (#5624)
source_pr.body:
After Close(), worker groutine receive from copIterator.finished and
exit directly, without writing any thing to taskCh.
Next() receives from taskCh and may hang forever, cause the caller goroutine
leak.


Cherry pick from release 1.0
@shenli @coocood

symptoms: rss, err := tk.Se.Execute("select * from earlyclose order by id") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: rss, err := tk.Se.Execute("select * from earlyclose order by id") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: rss, err := tk.Se.Execute("select * from earlyclose order by id") (executor/executor_test.go)
